codeReview.summary: Implementation satisfies the feature intent: a shared attachment response helper was added and all three targeted handlers now use it, removing duplicated response-builder blocks while preserving behavior.

issues.description: `attachment(...)` ends with `.expect("attachment responses should build")` in the request path, which can panic instead of returning a typed HTTP error. This is low-risk here because current call sites use static header values, but it still leaves a panic path in handlers.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission AGENTS says request-path code should avoid panic helpers ("Never unwrap() at request path; expect only in tests"), but this feature introduces `expect` in a handler helper. The convention may need clarification/enforcement because implementation and guidance diverge.

The rust-worker skill prescribes Red→Green with a failing test first, but this feature was marked as having followed procedure without adding/updating tests. The skill may be too rigid for pure dedup refactors, or compliance criteria should be clarified.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill requirement: .factory/skills/rust-worker/SKILL.md:31-32; handoff declares no new tests and still marks followedProcedure true at handoff lines 47 and 51-52 in 2026-04-18T00-51-45-545Z__m3-handler-attachment-helper__347c62f4-0703-4946-8614-a7dd7986f877.json

summary: Pass: the helper dedup is correctly implemented and wired into export_csv/save_pair_order/save_comparison_snapshot. One non-blocking panic-path concern and two shared-state alignment observations are recorded.
